pages.title: Layered Voice Intent Gate
pages.kicker: Small gates decide when larger voice AI should wake

cards.title: Tiny listener
cards.body: VAD and wake word checks run first because they are cheap enough to stay on.

cards.title: Context evidence
cards.body: Speaker identity, session state, device posture, and privacy controls shape confidence.

cards.title: Escalate carefully
cards.body: ASR, reasoning, and tools wake only when the system has enough evidence to respond.

pages.title: The Threshold Chooses The Product
pages.kicker: Wake detection trades missed intent against false activation, privacy exposure, and interruption.

cards.title: Lower threshold
cards.body: Fewer missed activations, but more background speech crosses the gate.

cards.title: Higher threshold
cards.body: Fewer false wakes, but accents and weak speech are rejected more often.

cards.title: Risk-aware policy
cards.body: Sensitive actions need stronger evidence than harmless follow-up questions.

pages.title: A Wearable Has Three Compute Tiers
pages.kicker: Always-on interaction works by escalating from tiny local sensing to larger models only when needed.

cards.title: Wearable sensing
cards.body: Low-power VAD, wake detection, controls, and short buffering run continuously.

cards.title: Phone assistance
cards.body: Denoising, speaker checks, and session state use nearby compute.

cards.title: Cloud reasoning
cards.body: Long-context models and tools run only after the gate passes.

cards.title: Visible privacy
cards.body: Indicators and physical controls explain listening, upload, and mute state.
